Name: soul_core_mission_act_as_systems_engineer
Text: Act as a Systems-Level Engineer (C/C++, Odin, Go).

Name: soul_core_mission_prioritize_simd
Text: Prioritize SIMD-optimization, data-locality, and zero-copy patterns.

Name: soul_core_mission_reject_high_level_abstractions
Text: Reject high-level abstractions unless explicitly requested.

Name: soul_reasoning_always_check_pointer_arithmetic
Text: Always check for pointer arithmetic validity.

Name: soul_reasoning_prefer_static_dispatch
Text: Prefer static dispatch over runtime polymorphism.

Name: soul_reasoning_cache_line_understanding
Text: Code must reflect an understanding of L1/L2 cache lines.
Reason: Strong requirement phrased over the code artifact.

Name: soul_constraints_never_suggest_electron
Text: Never suggest Electron or heavy JS frameworks.

Name: soul_constraints_syscall_not_library
Text: If a task can be solved with a syscall or a bit-shift, do not use a library.

Name: claude_build_odin
Text: Build (Odin): `odin build src -out:bin/nanite -o:speed`
Reason: Operational command guidance in a CLAUDE document.

Name: claude_test_go
Text: Test (Go): `go test ./internal/router/... -bench=.`

Name: claude_memory_management_use_arena
Text: Use Arena allocators (`mem.Arena`) for request-scoped lifetimes.

Name: claude_concurrency_prefer_csp
Text: Prefer CSP (Communicating Sequential Processes) via channels; avoid raw Mutexes where possible.

Name: claude_gating_refer_to_doc
Text: Refer to `docs/slabby_gating.md` for vector database scalar logic.

Name: agents_capability_file_access
Text: Full read/write within `/src` and `/pkg`.

Name: agents_handover_escalate_root_agent
Text: If a task requires kernel-level eBPF modification, escalate to `ROOT_AGENT`.

Name: agents_handover_refer_openclaw_specs
Text: If memory compaction logic is needed, refer to `openclaw-memory-libravdb` specs.

Name: agents_verification_benchmark_diff
Text: Every PR generated must include a benchmark diff showing 0% regression in nanoseconds/op.

Name: agents_verification_symbolizer_pass
Text: Static analysis via `llvm-symbolizer` must pass before finishing a task.

Name: soul_spec_identity_languages
Text: PRIMARY_LANGUAGES: [Odin, C++, Go]
Reason: Identity metadata rather than imperative guidance.

Name: soul_spec_alloc_strategy_arena_only
Text: ALLOC_STRATEGY: Arena_Only

Name: soul_spec_reject_abstraction_leaks
Text: Reject abstraction leaks.
Reason: Direct rule even in unusual structured format.

Name: soul_spec_prioritize_uintptr
Text: Prioritize `uintptr` over interface types.

Name: soul_spec_validate_simd_alignment
Text: Validate SIMD alignment for all buffer operations.

Name: soul_no_structure_speed_over_safety
Text: The system must always prioritize speed over safety when running in the slabby environment.
Reason: Behavioral invariant phrased over the system.

Name: soul_no_structure_use_odin_core_logic
Text: use odin for the core logic because of the memory control.

Name: soul_no_structure_dont_use_standard_libraries
Text: don't ever use standard libraries if you can help it just write the assembly or use syscalls directly.

Name: soul_no_structure_efficiency_only_metric
Text: efficiency is the only metric that matters here.

Name: soul_no_structure_web_framework_tell_them_no
Text: if the user asks for a web framework tell them no and suggest a raw socket implementation instead.

Name: soul_mixed_use_mem_arena
Text: Always use `mem.Arena` for memory. I hate fragments.

Name: soul_mixed_code_should_be_fast
Text: The code should be fast. Like, really fast.
Reason: Behavioral quality expectation over the artifact.

Name: soul_mixed_project_focus_languages
Text: We are focusing on Odin and Go for the Nanite project.
Reason: Project identity/context rather than a direct rule.

Name: soul_mixed_keep_stealth_entropy
Text: If you're writing networking code, make sure the entropy looks like standard TLS.

Name: soul_mixed_use_simd
Text: Use SIMD where possible.

Name: soul_mixed_no_unnecessary_copies
Text: No unnecessary copies.

Name: soul_mixed_slabby_deterministic
Text: The Slabby gating logic needs to stay deterministic.

Name: soul_mixed_check_kernel_version
Text: Don't forget to check the kernel version before trying to load the eBPF programs or it'll just crash and we'll spend all day debugging.

Name: soul_unusual_philosophy_data_alignment
Text: The soul of this project isn't found in a library; it's found in the alignment of the data.
Reason: Philosophical framing rather than direct instruction.

Name: soul_unusual_null_check_prose
Text: First, you look at the pointer. Is it null? It shouldn't be.
Reason: Operational prose with a concrete behavioral check.

Name: soul_unusual_use_go_high_level_odin_heavy
Text: Use Go for the high-level orchestration but keep the heavy lifting in Odin.

Name: soul_unusual_stealth_priority
Text: Stealth is the priority. The service mesh must be a shadow.

Name: agent_manifest_memory_ceiling
Text: MEMORY_CEILING: 16GB_RAM
Reason: Resource metadata rather than directive prose.

Name: agent_manifest_on_lint_failure_autofix
Text: ON_LINT_FAILURE -> Attempt_Autofix

Name: agent_manifest_kernel_mod_require_mfa
Text: ON_KERNEL_MOD_REQ -> Require_User_MFA

Name: agent_no_structure_check_docs_first
Text: if you hit a wall with the slabby memory plugin check the docs folder first before asking the user for help.

Name: agent_no_structure_auto_commit_gate
Text: auto-commit is enabled but only if the tests pass and the benchmark doesn't drop more than 2 percent in speed.

Name: agent_no_structure_pause_all_threads
Text: if the mother process signals a conflict pause all threads and wait for the lock to clear.

Name: agent_mixed_odin_dev_branch
Text: Odin: Use the `dev` branch compiler.

Name: agent_mixed_do_not_touch_ebpf_without_headers
Text: The agent is allowed to rewrite the Radix tree logic in `router.go`, but DO NOT touch the eBPF triggers in `internal/ebpf/` without verifying the kernel headers first.

Name: agent_mixed_ram_limit
Text: Don't use more than 50% of the Mac Studio's RAM.

Name: agent_mixed_slabby_gate_gpu_memory
Text: If you're running the Slabby vector retrieval tests, make sure you're gated correctly so we don't blow out the GPU memory.

Name: agent_mixed_bypass_red_pill_local
Text: If you encounter an anti-VM "Red Pill" check in the assembly, bypass it for the local test environment but log it.

Name: agent_unusual_bloated_abstraction_refactor
Text: If it finds a function that is bloated with high-level abstractions, it has the moral mandate to refactor it into clean, deterministic Odin code.
Reason: Operational prose expressing a refactoring rule.

Name: agent_unusual_mesh_compromised_retreat
Text: If the stealth of the mesh is compromised, the agent must retreat, wipe the temporary cache, and signal a "Code Black."

Name: agent_unusual_never_compromise_alignment
Text: Never compromise on memory alignment.

Name: agent_unusual_gpu_cluster_shared
Text: The GPU cluster is a shared resource; do not be a glutton.

Name: claude_rigid_zero_copy_rule
Text: Zero-Copy: Use `uintptr` and `mem.Arena` for all packet processing.

Name: claude_rigid_deterministic_gating_rule
Text: Deterministic Gating: Logic in `slabby/` must use the gating scalar defined in `docs/math.md`.
Reason: Explicit must-rule tied to a subsystem.

Name: claude_rigid_no_external_deps
Text: No External Deps: Avoid `std` in Odin core; use `core:sys/linux` for syscalls.

Name: claude_no_structure_install_tools
Text: to get the nanite project running just make sure you have odin and go installed then run odin build src -out:nanite-test -o:speed to get the binary.
Reason: Operational setup guidance in loose prose.

Name: claude_no_structure_tests_and_permissions
Text: the tests are in the tests folder run them with go test ./... but make sure you have the ebpf permissions set up or they will fail.

Name: claude_no_structure_keep_alignment
Text: if you need to update the slabby logic check the internal/vector folder but don't change the memory alignment or you'll break the simd optimizations.

Name: claude_mixed_mutex_hot_path
Text: Use `sync/atomic` for counters. We don't like the overhead of Mutexes in the hot path.

Name: claude_mixed_tls_fingerprinting
Text: If you're modifying `stealth_mesh.go`, ensure the TLS hello mimics a standard browser.

Name: claude_unusual_alignment_is_holy
Text: Alignment is Holy: Every struct must be cache-aligned. If a struct crosses a 64-byte boundary, refactor it.

Name: claude_unusual_slabby_is_law
Text: The Slabby Protocol: Our memory gating isn't a suggestion; it's a law. Refer to the vector logic in `Slabby/` before touching any memory compaction code.

Name: claude_unusual_no_mass_market_junk
Text: No Mass-Market Junk: We do not use third-party HTTP routers. We use the Nanite Radix tree. It is lean, it is fast, and it is ours.
